nodes.title: model
widgets_values:
I recommend this model (https://civitai.com/models/1981116?modelVersionId=2555640) preferably it's gguf version here (https://civitai.com/models/2269796?modelVersionId=2554882) 
Yes it is mostly for NSFW stuff but it works very good overall with everything. Usually doesn't need lora's (makes it run faster) 
Yes I know comfyui has been updated recently and supposed to be better with memory management when using the standard fp8 safetensors versions of the models but in my experience at best the fp8 version takes the same time but after a few gens it uses all the system ram and eventually starts to get slower. If you use a fixed gguf quant though and know what you are getting and what you are aiming for, you'll see you can get the same speed constantly and it won't fill the system ram as fast.That model has the lightning loras in it so need to have those kinds of loras. 

Regarding block swap :
Even if your model is small like say a q3 which is around 6-7 gb so it would fit on a 16gb vram for example. Still in my tests I saw that just enabling the block swap feature with it set to 20, it always helps with generation speed.

Regarding torch compile :
Try without it first (right click the node and bypass). It , while not as much as using sageattention , has a positive effect on gen time.

nodes.title: nodes needed
widgets_values:
* cfz-caching (from comfyui-zluda)
* kjnodes
* WanVideoWrapper
* comfyui-frame-interpolation (if you want to "interpolate" double your framerate in the end.)
* comfyui-easy-use

cfz-start :
helps with clearing terminal if you want, also if add it to other nodes, it will calculate how much time has passed between any two node or the full workflow. Finally can enable/disable cudnn.

resolution :
the number entered is applied to both height and width of input image and resized (multiples of 16, better for wan), or can use any resolution manually of course (always make sure multiples of 16)

length : 
81 is the highest with base model. (16x5+1) ,there are other methods models that can use higher framecounts. Always make sure it is in 4x+1 format.

total steps / half-step :
it divides total steps across high and low models, and you could change the half-step , it is not always the best for equal steps, so for example I sometimes use 3+1 , 1+1 , 3+2 ... etc.

prompt & negative :
these are made with my cfz-caching nodes. You can of course use the usual clip loader-clip text encode way for both positive and negative, but if you do lots of gens with same prompt or at least use the same negative , you can create it and save it with the group named -prompt creation- and then refresh the window so you can see it on the "prompt" or "negative". To just run and generate and save prompts in that group, fill the text encode, give it a name in save conditioning and "execute" the preview as text node. This will let you generate prompts again and again and later use them on the workflow. AND all of this means you don't need to spend additional vram, system ram or time to use the clip model.

nodes.title: "tiled vae"
widgets_values: this subgraph compares your input resolution and enabled tiled vae encoding / decoding if it is above a number you can change. (I use 640 , so over 640 it starts to tile)

nodes.title: if bigger than this enable tiled
